title: Write tomorrow's three priorities, close the notebook
subtitle: A two-minute wind-down activity for capturing the next day's most important tasks and physically closing the loop.
excerpt: A simple two-minute activity for Day 7 of the SLEEP program. Write three (and only three) priorities for tomorrow, then close the notebook. The day is complete.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. Developed as a companion practice to Day 7 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

content.text: A two-minute activity for Day 7 of the SLEEP program: the three things that matter most tomorrow, written by hand, and the notebook closed. Not a full plan, a handover to future-you, so tonight-you can stop.
A notebook or paper, not a screen
At the end of the working day, before you leave your desk and before you pick up your phone, open the notebook to a clean page.
Write tomorrow's date at the top.

Write three, and only three, priorities. Not a full to-do list. Three. The constraint is the practice. If you have ten things that feel urgent, ask: which three, if they were done, would make tomorrow feel complete? Write those.
The closing is not symbolic. It is the end. Tomorrow is held. Tonight is yours.
Original to homemade.education. Developed as a companion practice to Day 7 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
